dialogueChunks.intro: The situation is deteriorating, {player_name}. What started the {quest_focus} is spreading.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: The blight has expanded beyond the original area. The {enemy_type} are behaving strangely: moving in patterns, almost ritualistic. Worse, the {quest_item} you gathered isn't enough. {target_npc} found records describing a second component hidden {location_hint}, protected by something the texts call a 'warden.'
dialogueChunks.outro: We underestimated the {quest_threat}. It's not just angry. It's organized. The {quest_stakes} depends on finding the second component.

playerResponses.text: A warden doesn't scare me. Tell me where to find the second component.
This keeps getting bigger. I'll need significant compensation for taking on wardens.
Organized supernatural forces? I know when I'm outmatched.

dialogueChunks.intro: {player_name}, something spoke to {target_npc} last night. Through the walls of the shrine.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: {target_npc} won't repeat exactly what was said, but the message was clear: the {quest_threat} demands a tribute of {quest_item} at the {quest_focus}, or the punishment escalates. The {enemy_type} attacks have intensified as if enforcing the demand. The old records mention a place {location_hint} where a previous generation found a way to negotiate without submission.
dialogueChunks.outro: Submission or defiance: both have costs. We need a third option, and you might be the one to find it.

playerResponses.text: If there's a precedent for negotiation, I'll find it.
Ultimatums from ancient forces. What a time to be alive. I'm in.
Negotiating with divine forces is beyond any mortal's ability.

dialogueChunks.intro: It's inside the settlement now, {player_name}. Whatever we disturbed, it's not staying at the edges anymore.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: Buildings cracked from foundation to roof last night. The well tastes of iron and the {enemy_type} have breached the perimeter, drawn inward by the {quest_threat}. {target_npc} believes the original offense against the {quest_focus} can only be undone at the source: deep in the ruins {location_hint}. You'll need {quest_item} for the cleansing ritual.
dialogueChunks.outro: We can't run from this. The only way out is through. The {quest_stakes} won't survive the week otherwise.

playerResponses.text: Into the ruins, then. Whatever's down there, I'll face it.
Structural collapse and monsters inside the walls? This is dire. But I'll go.
When buildings start cracking, it's time to evacuate, not dig deeper.
